Name: Charmander
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: From the time it is born, a flame burns at the tip of its tail. Its life would end if the flame were to go out.

Name: Charmander
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: From the time it is born, a flame burns at the tip of its tail. Its life would end if the flame were to go out.

Name: Charmeleon
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may discard the top 5 cards of your deck. If any of those cards are [R] Energy cards, attach them to this Pokémon.
Effects: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When it swings its burning tail, it elevates the air temperature to unbearably high levels.

Name: Charmeleon
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may discard the top 5 cards of your deck. If any of those cards are [R] Energy cards, attach them to this Pokémon.
Effects: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When it swings its burning tail, it elevates the air temperature to unbearably high levels.

Name: Charizard
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon’s attacks do 30 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon for each of their Pokémon-GX and Pokémon-EX in play.
Effects: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its wings can carry this Pokémon close to an altitude of 4,600 feet. It blows out fire at very high temperatures.

Name: Torchic
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: A fire burns inside, so it feels very warm to hug. It launches fireballs of 1,800 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Combusken
Ability-Effect: Whenever you attach an Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon, remove all Special Conditions from it.
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: During a battle, the hot flame in its body increases. Its kicks have outstanding destructive power.

Name: Blaziken
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may attach a [R] Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Effects: Discard a [R] Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: When facing a tough foe, it looses flames from its wrists. Its powerful legs let it jump clear over buildings.

Name: Victini Prism Star
Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each basic Energy card in your discard pile. Then, shuffle those cards into your deck.
Prism-Star-Rule: You can’t have more than 1 [*] card with the same name in your deck. If a [*] card would go to the discard pile, put it in the Lost Zone instead.
Flavor-Text: When it shares the infinite energy it creates, that being’s entire body will be overflowing with power.

Name: Darumaka
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: When it sleeps, it pulls its limbs into its body and its internal fire goes down to 1,100 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Darmanitan
Effects: Attach up to 3 [R] Energy cards from your hand to your Pokémon in any way you like.
Flip 4 coins. This attack does 50 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Its internal fire burns at 2,500 degrees Fahrenheit, making enough power that it can destroy a dump truck with one punch.

Name: Heatmor
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon isn’t Burned, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It draws in air through its tail, transforms it into fire, and uses it like a tongue. It melts Durant and eats them.

Name: Reshiram-GX
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 [R] Energy cards and attach them to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
You may attach up to 5 [R] Energy cards from your hand to your Pokémon in any way you like. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Litten
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: While grooming itself, it builds up fur inside its stomach. It sets the fur alight and spews fiery attacks, which change based on how it coughs.

Name: Salandit
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Volcanoes or dry, craggy places are its home. It emanates a sweet-smelling poisonous gas that attracts bug Pokémon, then attacks them.

Name: Salazzle
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned. It can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Filled with pheromones, its poisonous gas can be diluted to use in the production of luscious perfumes.

Name: Horsea
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It makes its nest in the shade of corals. If it senses danger, it spits murky ink and flees.

Name: Horsea
Effects: This attack does 10 more damage times the amount of [W] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It makes its nest in the shade of corals. If it senses danger, it spits murky ink and flees.

Name: Seadra
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage times the amount of [W] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is capable of swimming backwards by rapidly flapping its winglike pectoral fins and stout tail.

Name: Kingdra-GX
Effects: This attack does 50 more damage times the amount of [W] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
This attack does 40 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Magikarp
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its reckless leaps make it easy pickings for predators. On the bright side, many Pokémon enjoy longer life spans, thanks to Magikarp.

Name: Gyarados
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent’s attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 2 damage counters on each of your Benched Pokémon.
Effects: You may discard any Stadium card in play.
Flavor-Text: There are people who swear that any place Gyarados appears is fated for destruction.

Name: Lapras
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
This attack does 10 more damage times the amount of [W] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon were once near extinction due to poaching. Following protective regulations, there is now an overabundance of them.

Name: Totodile
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Its powerful, well-developed jaws are capable of crushing anything. Even its Trainer must be careful.

Name: Croconaw
Effects: Discard the top 3 cards of your deck.
Flavor-Text: Once it bites down, it won’t let go until it loses its fangs. New fangs quickly grow into place.

Name: Feraligatr
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may discard a [W] Energy card from your hand.
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each [W] Energy card in your discard pile. Then, shuffle those cards into your deck.
Flavor-Text: When it bites with its massive and powerful jaws, it shakes its head and savagely tears its victim up.

Name: Wooper
Flavor-Text: When the temperature cools in the evening, they emerge from water to seek food along the shore.

Name: Wooper
Flavor-Text: When the temperature cools in the evening, they emerge from water to seek food along the shore.

Name: Quagsire
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may move a [W] Energy from 1 of your Benched Pokémon to your Active Pokémon.
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage times the amount of [W] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This carefree Pokémon has an easygoing nature. While swimming, it always bumps into boat hulls.

Name: Quagsire
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may move a [W] Energy from 1 of your Benched Pokémon to your Active Pokémon.
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage times the amount of [W] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This carefree Pokémon has an easygoing nature. While swimming, it always bumps into boat hulls.

Name: Corsola
Effects: This attack does 20 damage times the amount of [W] Energy attached to this Pokémon to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Pursued by Mareanie for the branches on its head, this Pokémon will sometimes snap its own branches off as a diversion while it escapes.

Name: Feebas
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).
Flavor-Text: Although unattractive and unpopular, this Pokémon’s marvelous vitality has made it a subject of research.

Name: Milotic
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
This attack does 40 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It lives at the bottom of clear lakes. In times of war, it shows itself, which soothes people’s minds and hearts.

Name: Phione
Ability-Effect: Your [W] Pokémon can’t be Confused. If those Pokémon are already Confused, remove that Special Condition.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It drifts in warm seas. It always returns to where it was born, no matter how far it may have drifted.

Name: Wishiwashi
Ability-Effect: Your Wishiwashi-GX in play get +20 HP, and their attacks do 20 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: It’s awfully weak and notably tasty, so everyone is always out to get it. As it happens, anyone trying to bully it receives a painful lesson.

Name: Trapinch
Effects: This attack does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: As it digs through the sand, its giant jaws crush any rocks that obstruct its path. It builds a funnel-shaped nest.

Name: Hydreigon
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may choose 3 of your Benched Pokémon. Then, discard your other Benched Pokémon.
Effects: You may discard an Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The heads on their arms do not have brains. They use all three heads to consume and destroy everything.

Name: Dratini
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: After a 10-hour struggle, a fisherman was able to pull one up and confirm its existence.

Name: Dratini
Flavor-Text: After a 10-hour struggle, a fisherman was able to pull one up and confirm its existence.

Name: Dragonair
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 60 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: From time immemorial, it has been venerated by agricultural peoples as an entity able to control the weather.

Name: Dragonite-GX
Effects: This Pokémon can’t attack during your next turn.
Put 3 [N] Pokémon from your discard pile onto your Bench. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Vibrava
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: To help make its wings grow, it dissolves quantities of prey in its digestive juices and guzzles them down every day.

Name: Flygon
Ability-Effect: Prevent all effects of your opponent’s attacks, except damage, done to your [N] Pokémon. (Existing effects are not removed.)
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon hides in the heart of sandstorms it creates and seldom appears where people can see it.

Name: Altaria
Ability-Effect: Your [N] Pokémon’s attacks do 20 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: On sunny days, it flies freely through the sky and blends into the clouds. It sings in a beautiful soprano.

Name: Altaria
Ability-Effect: Your [N] Pokémon’s attacks do 20 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: On sunny days, it flies freely through the sky and blends into the clouds. It sings in a beautiful soprano.

Name: Altaria
Ability-Effect: Your [N] Pokémon’s attacks do 20 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: On sunny days, it flies freely through the sky and blends into the clouds. It sings in a beautiful soprano.

Name: Altaria-GX
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Pokémon-GX and Pokémon-EX.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep. Heal all damage from all of your Pokémon. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Bagon
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: With its steel-hard stone head, it headbutts indiscriminately. This is because of the stress it feels at being unable to fly.

Name: Shelgon
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has any basic Energy attached to it, it takes 20 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: They lurk deep within caves—motionless, neither eating nor drinking. Why they don’t die is not known.

Name: Salamence-GX
Ability-Effect: Your Pokémon in play have no Retreat Cost, except Pokémon-GX and Pokémon-EX.
Effects: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.
This attack does 120 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Druddigon
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent’s attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 3 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It warms its body by absorbing sunlight with its wings. When its body temperature falls, it can no longer move.

Name: Zekrom
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon does 50 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Concealing itself in lightning clouds, it flies throughout the Unova region. It creates electricity in its tail.

Name: Kyurem
Effects: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It generates a powerful, freezing energy inside itself, but its body became frozen when the energy leaked out.

Name: White Kyurem-GX
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
If this Pokémon has any damage counters on it, this attack does 80 more damage.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned and Paralyzed. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Zygarde
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
If this Pokémon has any damage counters on it, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It’s thought to be monitoring the ecosystem. There are rumors that even greater power lies hidden within it.

Name: Turtonator
Effects: Discard any amount of [R] Energy from your Pokémon. This attack does 50 damage for each card you discarded in this way.
Flavor-Text: It gushes fire and poisonous gases from its nostrils. Its dung is an explosive substance and can be put to various uses.

Name: Drampa
Effects: Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your [N] Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is friendly to people and loves children most of all. It comes from deep in the mountains to play with children it likes in town.

Name: Jangmo-o
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 20 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: They live in mountains where no trace of humans can be detected. Jangmo-o grow little by little as they battle one another.

Name: Hakamo-o
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: It sheds and regrows its scales on a continuous basis. The scales become harder and sharper each time they’re regrown.

Name: Kommo-o
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
If this Pokémon has 8 or more damage counters on it, this attack does 120 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its rigid scales function as offense and defense. In the past, its scales were processed and used to make weapons and other commodities.

Name: Kangaskhan
Effects: Draw a card.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The child in its pouch leaves home after roughly three years. That is the only time the mother is heard to cry wildly.

Name: Swablu
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: It constantly grooms its cotton-like wings. It takes a shower to clean itself if it becomes dirty.

Name: Swablu
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: It constantly grooms its cotton-like wings. It takes a shower to clean itself if it becomes dirty.

Name: Swablu
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It constantly grooms its cotton-like wings. It takes a shower to clean itself if it becomes dirty.

Name: Blaine’s Last Stand
Description: You can play this card only when it is the last card in your hand.Draw 2 cards for each [R] Pokémon you have in play.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Dragon Talon
Description: If the [N] Pokémon this card is attached to is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent’s attack (even if that Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 3 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Fiery Flint
Description: You can play this card only if you discard 2 other cards from your hand.Search your deck for up to 4 [R] Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Fiery Flint

Name: Lance Prism Star
Description: You can play this card only if 1 of your Pokémon was Knocked Out during your opponent’s last turn.Search your deck for up to 2 [N] Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Switch Raft
Description: Switch your Active [W] Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, heal 30 damage from the Pokémon you moved to your Bench.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Wela Volcano Park
Description: Whenever a player flips a coin for the Special Condition Burned between turns, that Special Condition isn’t removed even if the result is heads.
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.
